title: Sequence Residual Model Over Last 5-8 Laps
summary: Recent pit-stop prediction work found sequential deep models strongest because pit calls depend on evolving trends, not one-row snapshots.
rationale: Recent pit-stop prediction work found sequential deep models strongest because pit calls depend on evolving trends, not one-row snapshots. The current solution is almost entirely tabular; a small sequence model can contribute orthogonal signal without replacing the main booster.
implementation_hint: Build per-driver race sequences using only the last 5-8 laps of features such as LapTime_Delta, Position_Change, TyreLife, Cumulative_Degradation, field-relative z-scores, and recent pit cooldown flags. Train a compact GRU/TCN/BiLSTM or even a 1D-CNN, then use strict OOF probabilities as one extra meta-feature for the tabular model.
expected_effect: Adds sensitivity to acceleration in wear and pace loss, improving recall on emerging pit windows that static ratios miss.
risk: Window construction can silently leak if sequences are built before fold splitting or if padding crosses pit resets/race boundaries.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
